intro: The biggest real split between Slack and Forem is lock-in posture: Slack carries medium lock-in (data exportable, ecosystem lock), while Forem carries low lock-in (oss community platform). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Slack is built for tech-forward companies needing a strong chat platform with a rich app ecosystem and is not a fit for microsoft-standardized orgs or teams prioritizing suite-bundled collaboration. Forem is built for engineering-led teams wanting a self-hosted dev.to-style community with full control and is not a fit for non-technical teams or anyone wanting a managed, zero-ops community platform. On the scoring side, Slack rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Forem rates 4/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Slack you accept that pricing feels punitive at scale, while Forem comes with the reality that self-host requires real rails ops. Neither side is free. Slack's headline strength in the data is that default chat for modern knowledge work.
quick_take: Slack is for tech-forward companies needing a strong chat platform with a rich app ecosystem; Forem is for engineering-led teams wanting a self-hosted dev.to-style community with full control; decide based.
verdict_a: Choose Slack if you are tech-forward companies needing a strong chat platform with a rich app ecosystem and can accept medium lock-in (data exportable, ecosystem lock).
verdict_b: Choose Forem if you are engineering-led teams wanting a self-hosted dev.to-style community with full control and need low lock-in (oss community platform).

faq.a: Slack uses a freemium model, while Forem uses a free model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Slack to Forem?
faq.a: Slack has medium lock-in (data exportable, ecosystem lock); Forem has low lock-in (oss community platform). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Slack; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
In our data, Slack scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Forem. That said, DX is subjective; the Forem score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Forem a good alternative to Slack?
faq.a: Forem is built for engineering-led teams wanting a self-hosted dev.to-style community with full control and explicitly not for non-technical teams or anyone wanting a managed, zero-ops community platform, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Slack for tech-forward companies needing a strong chat platform with a rich app ecosystem, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
